tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The `widgets` capability spec of record (`openspec/specs/widgets/spec.md`) still exists and contains full Listing behavior — including a "Filtering listings by category_id" scenario — even though the rename-to-listing change was archived. Meanwhile, `openspec/specs/listings/spec.md` only had a minimal list-count summary. The rename appears to have left the `widgets` spec populated and the `listings` spec nearly empty, creating duplication and an inconsistency in the spec of record.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The `widgets` spec of record is stale: its capability name and requirement titles still say "Widget" (e.g., "Creating a Widget", "Fetching a Widget"), but every scenario actually targets `/listings` paths and the `Listing` entity — a leftover from the rename-widget-to-listing change that was never cleaned up. Separately, `listings/spec.md` still carries a placeholder Purpose ("TBD - created by archiving change rename-widget-to-listing. Update Purpose after archive."). Neither affected the error-format task, but both are inaccuracies in the provided context.
tasks.nl_request: We're revising how the API communicates certain details to clients — bring it up to date.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: The main inaccuracy is documentation drift after the rename: `widgets/spec.md`'s Purpose still says "Widget endpoints" and its requirement names still say "Widget," even though all scenarios reference `/listings` paths. Also, `listings/spec.md`'s Purpose is still "TBD" — never updated after archiving. These are stale labels, not behavioral inaccuracies; the actual HTTP paths and field shapes in the specs are correct.
